name: Avakaya Mango Pickle
description: The legendary Andhra mango pickle — raw mango chunks slow-soaked in mustard, garlic and Guntur red chilli oil. Fierce, oily and unapologetically bold.

name: Gongura Pachadi
description: Tangy gongura (sorrel) leaves stone-pounded with red chillies, garlic and sesame — Andhra's most loved leafy pickle. Sharp, sour and deeply savoury.

name: Pandu Mirchi Nilava Pachadi
description: Fiery whole ripe-red chilli pickle, sun-cured in cold-pressed sesame oil with mustard and fenugreek. A spoonful is enough to light up a plate of hot rice.

name: Tomato Pachadi
description: Slow-cooked ripe tomatoes tempered with mustard, curry leaves and red chilli. Mellow, tangy and comforting — a gentler entry into Andhra pickles.

name: Usirikaya Amla Pickle
description: Gooseberry (amla) pickle pounded with mustard and chilli — bracingly sour, lightly bitter and full of goodness. A traditional winter favourite.

name: Nimmakaya Lemon Pickle
description: Sun-matured lemon pickle with salt, fenugreek and red chilli — bright, zesty and tangy. Softens beautifully over weeks of curing.

name: Guntur Mirchi Karam
description: Pure stone-ground Guntur red chilli powder — deep red colour, sharp aroma and unmistakable Andhra heat. The backbone of every Andhra kitchen.

name: Vellulli Karam
description: Garlic-rich karam podi — pounded with toasted chillies, garlic and salt. Spoon it over hot rice with a drizzle of ghee. Pungent and addictive.

name: Idli Karam Podi
description: Classic idli podi — roasted lentils, sesame and red chilli ground into a fragrant, spicy powder. The perfect partner for idli, dosa and ghee rice.

name: Kandi Podi
description: Toor dal podi — slow-roasted lentils with cumin, garlic and a whisper of chilli. Mild, nutty and wholesome; loved by kids and elders alike.

name: Kobbari Karam
description: Dry coconut podi — roasted coconut, chilli and tamarind ground into a sweet-spicy powder. Sprinkle over rice, dosa or upma for instant flavour.

name: Sambar Podi
description: Slow-roasted lentils, coriander seed and chilli — a fragrant blend that builds restaurant-style sambar at home. Aromatic, balanced and warming.
